title: Continual learning of context-dependent processing in neural networks
methods: network capacity analysis via rank of orthogonal projector

data_scope.MNIST: 70,000 images (60k train + 10k test), 10 digit classes, up to 100 sequential tasks
data_scope.CIFAR-10: 60,000 32x32 colour images, 10 classes, 5 sequential tasks (2 classes each)
data_scope.ImageNet_ILSVRC2012: approx. 1.28 million training images, 1,000 object classes, 1,000 sequential tasks
data_scope.CASIA_HWDB1_1: 3,755 Chinese character classes (level I vocabulary, >99% usage frequency), tested with fewer than 10 samples per class
data_scope.CelebA: over 200,000 face images, 40 binary facial attributes, 19,962 test images used in mixed-selectivity analysis

core_conclusion: An Orthogonal Weights Modification (OWM) algorithm prevents catastrophic forgetting by constraining weight updates to the subspace orthogonal to all previously learned inputs, enabling a single compact neural network to sequentially learn thousands of tasks (3,755 Chinese character classes; 1,000 ImageNet classes) with as few as ~10 samples per new task. Combined with a prefrontal-cortex-inspired Context-Dependent Processing (CDP) module that rotates the sensory feature space according to contextual signals, the system can apply different mapping rules to identical inputs across 40 distinct contexts, approaching multi-task training performance without replay buffers or network expansion.
why_it_matters: Provides a scalable, mathematically grounded solution to catastrophic forgetting without data replay or network growth; achieves ~92% accuracy on 3,755 sequential Chinese character classes approaching human performance (~96%); demonstrates primate-like cognitive flexibility in a compact DNN; bridges neuroscience (PFC mixed selectivity) and AI; fully open-source.
why_nature: Addresses a decades-long landmark open problem in neural networks; explicitly bridges AI and neuroscience via PFC-inspired architecture and mixed-selectivity analysis; demonstrates generality across five distinct domains (digits, colour images, natural images, Chinese handwriting, face attributes); compact systems with outsized capability matches Nature Machine Intelligence editorial preference for broadly impactful AI advances.
added_value_to_my_research: OWM as continual fine-tuning algorithm for geospatial foundation models across evolving urban contexts — retaining spatial patterns from earlier cities when adapting to new ones
CDP module as context-switching spatial prediction layer — replacing static GWR/MGWR with a neural analogue conditioned on spatial-coordinate or urban-typology context embeddings
OWM + plasticity-maintenance pairing for long-horizon spatiotemporal models (SIR epidemic updating) that must retain older patterns while remaining plastic to new dynamics
CDP-equipped accessibility model conditioned on urban-typology context — single model adaptable to any city type, replacing separate 2SFCA/R2SFCA parameterizations per study area
OWM curriculum sensitivity analysis — principled data-ordering strategy for multi-source geospatial data fusion (satellite, social media, POI, mobile phone) to maximize retained spatial structure

related.title: Loss of plasticity in deep continual learning
related.relation: identifies loss of plasticity as a separate failure mode from catastrophic forgetting, complementing OWM's retention-focused approach

related.title: Three types of incremental learning
related.relation: taxonomizes continual learning scenarios (task-incremental, class-incremental, domain-incremental) providing a formal framework that contextualizes where OWM excels

related.title: Engineering flexible machine learning systems by traversing functionally invariant paths
related.relation: explores weight-space paths that preserve task performance — a complementary geometric perspective to OWM's orthogonal-subspace projection

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: shares the brain-inspired neural dynamics motivation and explores biologically plausible spiking networks as an alternative continual-learning substrate

related.title: A foundation model for the Earth system
related.relation: demonstrates foundation model continual adaptation for Earth-system prediction — a geospatial deployment scenario where OWM-style fine-tuning would prevent catastrophic forgetting across climate contexts

figures.caption: Schematic of OWM — new task weight modifications are projected onto the subspace orthogonal to previously learned inputs, preventing interference.
OWM vs. CAB vs. SGD on 10-task disjoint MNIST — test accuracy as a function of classes learned, showing OWM's superior retention.
Few-shot continual learning on CASIA-HWDB1.1 — accuracy vs. pre-training extent, sample size, and network capacity (rank of orthogonal projector).
CDP module architecture and performance on CelebA — sequential learning of 40 facial attributes using a single classifier, approaching multi-task training accuracy.
